meta.title: AKLAN
meta.subtitle: Aklan, Western Visayas
meta.description: Aklan is a province in the Western Visayas region of the Philippines, best known as the gateway to Boracay Island. It is also famous for the Ati-Atihan Festival, one of the country's oldest and most celebrated cultural events, honoring the Santo Niño with colorful costumes and lively street dancing.

attractions.name: Bakhawan Ecopark
attractions.description: A stunning mangrove forest with boardwalks winding through lush greenery.

attractions.name: Saint John the Baptist Parish
attractions.description: A centuries-old church at the heart of Kalibo city, rich in heritage.

attractions.name: New Washington Beach
attractions.description: A peaceful local beach away from the tourist crowds.

attractions.name: Libacao Falls
attractions.description: Cascading waterfalls nestled in the mountains of Libacao town.

attractions.name: Tinagong Dagat
attractions.description: A hidden lake surrounded by mangroves, perfect for birdwatching.

attractions.name: Pat-Pay Shrine
attractions.description: A scenic hilltop shrine offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.

attractions.name: Aklan Museum
attractions.description: Home to cultural artifacts and exhibits about Aklanon history and tradition.

attractions.name: Barangay Bisita Chapel
attractions.description: A quiet roadside chapel known for its intricate craftsmanship.

attractions.name: Aklan River Cruise
attractions.description: Cruise along the peaceful Aklan River and enjoy the natural scenery.

activities.name: Ati-Atihan Festival
activities.description: Join the Philippines' most celebrated street festival with tribal dances and costumes.

activities.name: Mangrove Kayaking
activities.description: Paddle through the mangrove channels of Bakhawan Ecopark.

activities.name: Bamboo Weaving Workshop
activities.description: Learn traditional Aklanon bamboo weaving from local artisans.

activities.name: Island Hopping Buruanga
activities.description: Explore hidden coves and sandbars along the Buruanga coastline.

activities.name: Zip-Line Adventure
activities.description: Soar above the treetops on an exhilarating zip-line experience.

activities.name: Organic Farm Tour
activities.description: Visit local organic farms and learn about sustainable agriculture in Aklan.

activities.name: Mountain Biking Trail
activities.description: Ride through scenic mountain trails with views of the Aklan countryside.

activities.name: Bird Watching Tour
activities.description: Spot over 80 endemic bird species in the mangroves and forests.

activities.name: Pottery Making Class
activities.description: Create your own Aklanon-style pottery guided by local craftspeople.

food.name: Inubarang
food.description: A hearty local stew made with chicken and bamboo shoots, a proud Aklanon specialty.

food.name: Binakol
food.description: Chicken soup cooked inside a bamboo tube with coconut water and lemongrass.

food.name: Piaya
food.description: Flat unleavened bread filled with muscovado sugar, Aklan's iconic pasalubong.

food.name: Seafood sa Puso
food.description: Fresh grilled seafood served in banana leaf cones, a local dining experience.

food.name: Suman sa Lihiya
food.description: Sticky rice cake wrapped in banana leaves, a traditional Filipino delicacy.

food.name: KBL (Kadyos Baboy Langka)
food.description: A hearty Western Visayas soup of pigeon peas, pork, and jackfruit.

food.name: Garlic Chicharon
food.description: Crunchy pork rinds seasoned with native garlic, a popular Aklan street snack.

food.name: Baye-Baye
food.description: Roasted rice and coconut candy roll, a classic Aklanon sweet treat.

food.name: Fresh Tuba
food.description: Freshly tapped coconut wine, a beloved traditional drink of Western Visayas.

events.name: Ati-Atihan Festival
events.description: Third week of January — the Philippines' oldest street festival honoring Santo Niño.

events.name: Aklan Foundation Day
events.description: April 25 — province-wide celebrations with cultural shows and exhibits.

events.name: Higantes Parade
events.description: October — giant papier-mâché figures parade through the streets of Kalibo.

events.name: Kalibo Town Fiesta
events.description: August 29 — feast of Saint John the Baptist with processions and local games.

events.name: Aklan Farmers Festival
events.description: March — celebrating Aklan's agricultural heritage with fairs and produce displays.

events.name: Bamboo Summit
events.description: Annual event showcasing bamboo crafts and sustainable building from Aklan artisans.

events.name: Kaplag Festival
events.description: November — a thanksgiving harvest festival featuring indigenous Ati dances.

events.name: Mangrove Festival
events.description: May — environmental event with tree-planting drives and eco-tours at Bakhawan.

events.name: Piaya Food Festival
events.description: December — food fair celebrating Aklan's famous piaya and other delicacies.

accommodations.name: Boracay Beach Club
accommodations.description: Beachfront resort on Boracay with pools and world-class amenities.

accommodations.name: Kalibo Grand Hotel
accommodations.description: Modern hotel in the heart of Kalibo city, close to the airport.

accommodations.name: Aklan Nature Lodge
accommodations.description: Eco-lodge surrounded by lush greenery perfect for nature lovers.

accommodations.name: Mangrove Inn
accommodations.description: Charming guesthouse adjacent to Bakhawan Ecopark with river views.

accommodations.name: Casa Kalibo Boutique Hotel
accommodations.description: Boutique hotel with local art décor and modern comforts in Kalibo.

accommodations.name: Sunrise Beach Resort
accommodations.description: Family resort on a quiet Aklan beach with direct water access.

accommodations.name: Mountain Breeze Inn
accommodations.description: Hilltop inn offering cool breezes and stunning views of Aklan valley.

accommodations.name: Festival Hostel Kalibo
accommodations.description: Budget-friendly hostel popular with backpackers near the festival grounds.

accommodations.name: Hacienda Aklan
accommodations.description: Spanish colonial-style estate offering private villas and farm experiences.
